title: RAG Knowledge Base
summary: Same question answered with vs without retrieval. Left: RagPipeline ingests a curated corpus, retrieves the top-K hybrid (vector + BM25) passages, and synthesises a cited answer with the eval-winning defaults. Right: raw LLM with no retrieval — fluent but ungrounded.
description: RagPipeline is the new high-level RAG facade in swarmai-core. It bakes in the configuration that won the IntelliDoc 2026-04-26 evaluation against LangGraph-Python and LangChain4j-Java: chunk size 800/100, top-K 5, hybrid retrieval (vector + BM25 → RRF), simple LangGraph-style prompt, temperature 0.2, num_predict 350. The swarm side ingests a small markdown corpus and answers the prompt with [source: file #N] citations. The baseline side calls the same model with no corpus access — it can describe RAG generically but cannot cite the specific defaults this corpus documents.
asymmetryClaim: Cited answer pinned to the corpus vs a confident summary that can't reproduce a single specific default value.
